commonIssues: Frost-tender: plants are killed by light frost; do not plant outdoors until soil reaches 21C (70F) at 1-inch depth.
Standard varieties need insect (bee) pollination for fruit set; poor pollination causes misshapen or dropped fruit. Parthenocarpic hybrids set fruit without pollinators.
Heavy feeder for water: needs ~1 inch (25mm) of water per week; uneven moisture causes bitter or misshapen fruit.
Pests: striped/spotted cucumber beetles, aphids, spider mites, pickleworms, squash vine borers.
Diseases: powdery and downy mildew, bacterial wilt (vectored by cucumber beetles), anthracnose, angular leaf spot, mosaic viruses.
Best grown on a 3-6 ft trellis; container minimum is a 5-gallon (19L) pot with 1-2 plants and a support.

source.citation: University of Minnesota Extension — Growing cucumbers in home gardens: ~1 inch of water per week; sow when soil is at least 70F at 1-inch depth; plants cannot survive freezing; thin to 8-12 inches apart; use a 3-to-4-foot trellis; soil pH 6.0-6.5.

University of Minnesota Extension — Growing cucumbers in home gardens: frost-tender (plants cannot survive freezing temperatures), plant after last frost; soil pH 6.0-6.5; recommends a 3-4 ft trellis.

Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC — Cucumber: best growth between 75 and 85F; minimum planting soil temp 70F, no germination below 50F; very tender, killed by light frosts; harvest about 50-70 days; pre-plant 5-10-10 fertilizer, sidedress nitrogen; trellis about 6 feet high; most varieties require pollinating insects, parthenocarpic hybrids do not.
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC — Cucumber: pre-plant fertilizer 5-10-10 at 3 lb per 100 sq ft, then sidedress nitrogen before vine development; soil pH 6.0-6.5.

The Old Farmer's Almanac — Growing Cucumbers in Pots, Containers, Bags: at least a 5-gallon (about 19L) container, minimum 12 inches wide and 12 inches deep, 1-2 plants per pot; fresh potting/soilless mix; trellis recommended.

University of Illinois Extension / UGA Extension — cucumbers require long warm days and plenty of sunshine (full sun). Extensions describe the requirement as 'full sun' rather than a precise hours figure; targetSunHours 8 and minSunHours 6 reflect the standard full-sun definition (6+ hours/day).

Confidence note — lightProfile (targetSunHours/min/maxSunHours): authoritative extensions specify 'full sun' / 'plenty of sunshine' for cucumber but do not state an exact hours-per-day number. Values are conservative estimates derived from the standard horticultural definition of full sun (6+ hours direct sun); target 8h, max 14h reflect summer daylength, not a cited cap.

Confidence note — wateringProfile.baseIntervalDays and hotWeatherMultiplier: extensions specify ~1 inch (25mm) of water per week and warn cucumbers are heavy water users with low drought tolerance, but do not give a fixed day interval. A 2-day base interval (and 1.5x in hot weather) is a conservative conversion of 'consistent, uniform moisture, ~1 inch/week, more in heat' for a container/raised-bed care schedule.

Confidence note — feedingProfile intervals: extensions describe pre-plant fertilizer plus nitrogen sidedressing 'when plants begin to vine' / about one week after bloom then ~3 weeks later, not a fixed recurring day interval. A 14-day base/fruiting interval is a conservative schedule consistent with that cadence; preferredNpk 5-10-10 follows the Clemson pre-plant recommendation (avoid excess nitrogen, which reduces fruiting).
